content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Scaling Apps to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how to scale your apps and overcome debugging bottlenecks. A beginner-level tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster for clients.
content.answer_block: Debugging failure often stems from monolithic, unscalable architecture. By refactoring into decoupled, modular services using Antigravity and Claude Code, you isolate faults and simplify troubleshooting. This approach transforms a chaotic codebase into a predictable, scalable system, allowing you to ship professional-grade software with significantly reduced maintenance overhead.
content.intro:
You’re staring at the screen at 2:00 AM, the client’s deadline looming, and a cryptic error message is mocking your progress. You aren't just stuck; you’re trapped in a fragile codebase that breaks every time you touch it. This is the 'debugging wall'—the silent killer of freelance careers.

But what if you could stop fighting your code and start scaling it? By shifting your architecture toward modular, AI-assisted development, you move from manual bug-hunting to automated, resilient growth. It’s time to stop patching holes and start building an engine that runs itself.

steps.title: Analyze and Map the Monolith
steps.description: Identify the core bottleneck in your current codebase that makes debugging impossible. Use Antigravity to visualize dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase for high-coupling modules. Generate a dependency graph and identify the top 3 files causing circular dependencies or excessive side effects.

steps.title: Isolate Logic into Micro-Services
steps.description: Deconstruct the monolithic mess into discrete, testable functions to ensure that one failure doesn't cascade.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified problematic modules into independent, pure functions. Ensure data flow is unidirectional and state is isolated.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor src/api/handler.js into modular services'

steps.title: Implement Automated Health Checks
steps.description: Standardize your error handling so that the system reports failures before you even see them.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unified error handling wrapper that logs context, stack trace, and user-state to a centralized monitoring service.

steps.title: Automate Documentation with Claude
steps.description: Codify your new architecture so your future self—or your client—isn't left guessing how it works.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive technical README based on the new modular structure, including instructions on how to extend each service.

steps.title: Deploy to a Scalable Environment
steps.description: Move from local testing to a production-ready infrastructure that handles scaling automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Terraform or Docker-compose configuration optimized for horizontal scaling of the newly decoupled services.

pro_tips: Always use a 'debug-first' branch in Git to isolate experimental fixes from your stable production release.
Treat environment variables as immutable configuration to avoid 'it works on my machine' syndrome.
Use Claude Code to write tests before the code; it forces you to define success before you start typing.
Implement log aggregation early; debugging is 90% visibility and 10% coding.
Automate your dependency updates to avoid security-related bugs that are notoriously hard to trace.
content.next_steps: Once your modular architecture is live, implement a CI/CD pipeline to automate your testing suite, ensuring that every push is verified against your new standards.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting messy code dictate your output. Scale your development, impress your clients, and reclaim your time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
